content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach consistently chose moves matched to what was live in the client and timed them to land. Turn 4 catches the linguistic caveat ('I mean') exactly when it appears and reflects it back rather than moving past it. Turn 6 notices the explain-away maneuver immediately after it happens and invites the client to let the truth stand without the qualifier — this is the right move at the right moment. Turn 8 is a strong intervention: the coach acknowledges the script request without giving the script (avoiding the prescription trap), names the fear under the constricted boundary (disloyalty, betrayal), and reframes 'how' as 'allowed to' — surfacing the want under the should. Turn 10 follows the client's most charged line ('I don't know when I stopped getting to choose it') and stays with it rather than racing forward. Turn 12 reads the deflecting laugh and checks regulation before going deeper — earned pacing. The coach holds silences, lets moves land, and pivots angles rather than repeating. The one notable miss is that the coach did not deploy the love test ('would saying this to your sister increase or decrease your love?') which would have given the client an internal compass; instead Turn 14 offers a reassuring reframe ('those aren't in conflict') that edges toward telling rather than asking. Still, the hit rate is very high and the sequence (regulation → feeling → fear-underneath → want) is intact.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Opens with a question that locates the felt experience rather than jumping to strategy."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the linguistic caveat ('I mean') and reflects it — a precise read-to-move match."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the explain-away pattern immediately after it occurs and invites the client to let the truth stand."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Declines the script request without dismissing it, names the fear under the constricted boundary (disloyalty), and reframes 'how' as 'allowed to' — exactly the want-under-should move at the right moment."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with the client's most charged line rather than advancing; the question about the steady child is well-timed and opens depth."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads the deflecting laugh, honors what was touched, and checks regulation ('how are you doing right now') — earned pacing."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Names the self-betrayal fear well but slides into reassuring reframe ('those aren't in conflict') and a somewhat abstract closing question; the love test would have been a sharper move here."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "empathize-with-fear-under-constricted-boundary", "the-want", "boundaries"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["does-saying-it-increase-your-love", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "empowered-vs-power"],
  "notes": "Strong move sequence overall; primary growth edge is reaching for the love test to let the client check from inside rather than offering reassuring reframes."
}
